title: Retire SonarCloud

tasks.title: Reconcile security-gates docs to 'Sonar retired'

ROADMAP.md: status block refreshed (B archived, web #92, tag-only, test-tier in flight, Sonar retiring); B/C milestones marked done; added 'Test-tier restructure (in flight)' + 'SonarCloud retirement' sections with the 4 followups and the 'properties-removal-doesn't-stop-analysis' note.

tasks.title: Remove Sonar config from app repos (cross-project PRs)
tasks.verification: App-repo Sonar config removed via PRs: pokeedge #277 (rm sonar-project.properties + removed SONAR_TOKEN row in CI_CD_PIPELINE.md + reframed code-quality-coverage.md to 'SonarCloud retired'); web #94 (rm sonar-project.properties). Both auto-merge(squash) armed; land when Sharperflow CI Gate greens. No native security-gates changes (cross-project PRs).

tasks.title: Stop analysis + delete secret (privileged; execute-if-scoped else handoff)
tasks.verification: SONAR_TOKEN secret deleted from both repos (verified gh secret list shows none). App-disconnect + Automatic-Analysis disable documented as a runbook (SonarCloud project Admin → Analysis Method disable → unbind projects → org-owner uninstall sonarqubecloud App, in that order to avoid phantom binding) — requires SonarCloud admin + GitHub org-owner UI, NOT available from this session → handed off. App id 102484948 still installed (the remaining human step).

tasks.title: Verify Sonar retirement
tasks.verification: actionlint PASS (native); ci-standard.md + ROADMAP.md state Sonar retired + 4 followups; app PRs #277 (pokeedge) + #94 (web) both remove sonar-project.properties (auto-merge armed); SONAR_TOKEN gone from both repos. AC3 NOT fully met by automation: sonarqubecloud App id 102484948 STILL INSTALLED → Automatic Analysis still running until org-owner uninstalls + disables (documented runbook handed off). AC1/AC2/AC4/AC5 satisfied; AC3 = documented-handoff per agreement.

proposal.approval_evidence: User approved batch. Full Sonar retirement: disconnect sonarqubecloud App, remove sonar-project.properties (both repos) + SONAR_TOKEN, reconcile ROADMAP/ci-standard to 'no Sonar'. Parent of 4 capability-gap fast-follows. Queued at discovery.

discovery.approval_evidence: Agreement approved ('approve'); contract minted (10 items). Footprint: App id 102484948 active (push+PR events, write perms → Automatic Analysis + PR decoration on); sonar-project.properties both repos; SONAR_TOKEN both repos; legacy doc refs in pokeedge. App-repo edits + App/secret removal are privileged/cross-project. Design must resolve App-disconnect mechanism + cross-project routing.

design.approval_evidence: External mechanics validated vs Sonar docs: sonar-project.properties is IGNORED under Automatic Analysis (removal = hygiene, not stop); analysis stops only via SonarCloud Project Admin disable + GitHub App uninstall; safe order = unbind projects THEN uninstall App (phantom-binding gotcha). Design split: (A) native docs reconcile (ci-standard + ROADMAP) full-checkpoint here; (B) app-repo hygiene (remove sonar-project.properties both repos + pokeedge doc cleanup) via small plain-git PRs; (C) privileged stop-analysis runbook (SonarCloud admin + org-owner App uninstall) + SONAR_TOKEN deletion — handoff/execute-if-scoped. Key risk: file/secret removal alone does NOT stop analysis — AC3 not satisfied until step C executed. No novel architecture; external surface verified — no separate validator spawn.

planning.approval_evidence: User approved 4-task prep contract. T1 docs reconcile (native), T2 app-repo Sonar config removal (cross-repo PRs), T3 secret delete + stop-analysis runbook (privileged), T4 verify. AC3 satisfied only by actual analysis-stop, not file removal.

execution.approval_evidence: All 4 tasks checkpointed. Done: docs reconciled (ci-standard + ROADMAP), app PRs #277/#94 (sonar-project.properties removed, auto-merge armed), SONAR_TOKEN deleted both repos. AC1/AC2/AC4/AC5 met; AC3 (stop analysis) = documented runbook handed off — sonarqubecloud App still installed, org-owner uninstall + Automatic-Analysis disable pending (not doable from this session). actionlint PASS.

acceptance.approval_evidence: User uninstalled the sonarqubecloud App (verified: no longer in org installations) and directed 'remove sonar completely now and then continue'. SonarCloud fully retired: App gone, sonar-project.properties removed (PRs #277/#94), SONAR_TOKEN deleted both repos, docs reconciled. Contract 10/10 (AC3 + DONT2 flipped to satisfied after the uninstall). 4 capability-gap followups queued.

documents.proposal:
## Why
SonarCloud is half-removed (workflows gone, but the `sonarqubecloud` App + `sonar-project.properties` still live → Automatic Analysis still runs). The org chose full retirement. This change finishes it and records the true state + the capability-gap followups.

## What Changes
1. **Disconnect the `sonarqubecloud` GitHub App** from the org / both repos (org-admin op; documented runbook — App removal is not a repo file change).
2. **Remove `sonar-project.properties`** from PokeEdge and PokeEdge-Web `main` (cross-repo).
3. **Remove `SONAR_TOKEN` references** + the legacy mention in pokeedge `CI_CD_PIPELINE.md`; (secret deletion is an org/repo-admin op, documented).
4. **Reconcile the standard docs:** `docs/ci-standard.md` + `ROADMAP.md` — state "no Sonar" as current reality (not just a non-goal), refresh phase status (Changes B done, C done via #92, test-tier restructure in flight, tag-only release standard), and record the four capability-gap followups.

## Success Criteria
- `sonar-project.properties` absent from both app repos' `main`; no `SONAR_TOKEN`/Sonar references remain in CI configs/docs (except a historical note).
- `sonarqubecloud` App disconnected (verified via `gh api orgs/.../installations`), or the exact org-admin steps documented + handed off if the token lacks scope.
- ROADMAP.md reflects actual phase state + the 4 gap followups; ci-standard.md says Sonar is retired.

## Affected Code
PokeEdge `sonar-project.properties`, PokeEdge-Web `sonar-project.properties`, pokeedge `docs/CI_CD_PIPELINE.md`; security-gates `ROADMAP.md` + `docs/ci-standard.md`. Org: `sonarqubecloud` App + `SONAR_TOKEN` secret (admin ops).

## Related Repositories
Cross-repo: PokeEdge + PokeEdge-Web (properties removal) coordinated from the standard repo.

## Constraints
- Do not remove a Sonar capability the team still wants WITHOUT its gap-followup being tracked (the 4 followups cover this).
- App/secret removal are privileged org-admin ops — execute if token allows, else document + hand off.

## Discovery Agenda
- Confirm whether Sonar Automatic Analysis is on (App-level) vs CI-only, so disconnecting the App fully stops analysis.
- Confirm no other repo/script depends on `SONAR_TOKEN`.
- Sequence cross-repo property removal (coordinate with active peer agents in both app repos).

## Scope
### In Scope
sonar-project.properties removal (both repos), SONAR_TOKEN/doc cleanup, App-disconnect runbook, ROADMAP + ci-standard reconciliation.
### Out of Scope
Building replacements for the 4 lost capabilities (each is its own followup change); any new tool adoption.
### Must Not
Leave Sonar half-retired (App on, files off) — that is the current broken state; either fully disconnect or document the exact remaining admin step. Do not silently drop capabilities without a tracked followup.
documents.problemStatement:
SonarCloud is half-removed: the CI workflows (`sonarcloud.yml`, `ci-quality.yml`) are gone from both app repos, but the `sonarqubecloud` GitHub App (org id 102484948) is still installed and `sonar-project.properties` remains on `main` in both PokeEdge and PokeEdge-Web (with org `sharper-flow`, `sonarcloud.io`, and rule-ignore tuning). With the App connected and no CI analysis, SonarCloud Automatic Analysis keeps running on every push/PR. pokeedge's `CI_CD_PIPELINE.md` even documents `SONAR_TOKEN` as "legacy; SonarCloud workflow is currently disabled."

The org has decided to **fully retire SonarCloud** (posture: no hosted dashboard, no Sonar) now that the high-signal OSS gates own the required security + coverage gate. Retiring Sonar makes four of its capabilities genuinely absent — each will be handled by a dedicated followup change (duplication detection; maintainability/reliability ratings + tech-debt; coverage-on-new-code + trends/PR-decoration; deep dataflow/taint SAST).

Desired outcome: SonarCloud is cleanly and fully retired — App disconnected, `sonar-project.properties` + `SONAR_TOKEN` references removed from both repos — and the standard docs/roadmap reflect the true state (no Sonar) plus the four capability-gap followups.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement — retireSonarcloud

## Findings
- `sonarqubecloud` App (id 102484948) installed with push+pull_request events and pull_requests/statuses/checks/security_events write → Automatic Analysis + PR decoration active. Disconnecting the App stops it.
- Footprint: `sonar-project.properties` on both repos' main; `SONAR_TOKEN` secret in both repos; legacy refs in pokeedge `CI_CD_PIPELINE.md` + `code-quality-coverage.md`. (sonarcloud.yml already gone from main; search index stale.)
- App-repo files/secrets are cross-project; only the standard docs are native to security-gates.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **AC1** `sonar-project.properties` removed from PokeEdge + PokeEdge-Web `main`.
- **AC2** `SONAR_TOKEN` secret removed from both repos; legacy Sonar references cleaned from pokeedge `docs/CI_CD_PIPELINE.md` + `docs/code-quality-coverage.md`.
- **AC3** `sonarqubecloud` App disconnected (Automatic Analysis stops) — verified via `gh api orgs/Sharper-Flow/installations` no longer listing it; OR the exact org-admin / SonarCloud-side disconnect steps documented and handed off if the session token lacks scope.
- **AC4** security-gates `ROADMAP.md` + `docs/ci-standard.md` reconciled: Sonar retired (current reality, not just a non-goal), phase status refreshed (B done, C done via #92, test-tier restructure in flight, tag-only release standard), and the 4 capability-gap followups recorded.
- **AC5** No Sonar references remain in CI configs/workflows; `actionlint` + self-test green.

## Constraints
- App disconnect, secret deletion, and app-repo file edits are privileged/cross-project ops — execute where the token + project context allow, else document the exact steps and hand off.
- Coordinate app-repo edits with the active peer agents in PokeEdge / PokeEdge-Web.
- Removing `sonar-project.properties` is safe — nothing in the required `Sharperflow CI Gate` path reads it.

## Avoidances
- Do not leave Sonar half-retired (App on, files off) — fully disconnect or document the exact remaining admin step.
- Do not drop a wanted Sonar capability without its tracked followup (the 4 fast-follows cover duplication, maintainability, diff-coverage, deep SAST).

## Verify in design
- Exact App-disconnect mechanism: GitHub org settings (Installed Apps) vs SonarCloud project admin (disable Automatic Analysis) vs API; confirm token scope.
- Cross-project execution routing for the PokeEdge / PokeEdge-Web file + secret removals (native app sessions vs target_path edits + manual commits).
documents.design:
# Design — retireSonarcloud

External mechanics validated against Sonar docs (docs.sonarsource.com Automatic Analysis + Changing-organization-binding):
- Under Automatic Analysis, SonarCloud **ignores** `sonar-project.properties` — so removing it is hygiene, NOT a stop mechanism.
- Analysis stops only via **SonarCloud Project Admin → Analysis Method → disable Automatic Analysis**, and/or **uninstalling the `sonarqubecloud` GitHub App** (unbinds the org).
- **Safe order:** remove each project's binding in SonarCloud FIRST, then uninstall the GitHub App — otherwise projects keep a stale "phantom" ALM binding that breaks re-binding/PR decoration.
- Org-wide auto-disable is Enterprise-only → use per-project disable.

## Execution split

### A. Native (security-gates) — fully done here, checkpointed (AC4, AC5)
- `docs/ci-standard.md`: state Sonar is **retired** (current reality); the no-Sonar/no-hosted-dashboard posture stands; record the 4 capability-gap followups (`addDuplicationDetection`, `addMaintainabilityMetrics`, `addDiffCoverageGate`, `deepenSastDataflow`) and that duplication/maintainability/diff-coverage/deep-SAST are tracked, not silently dropped.
- `ROADMAP.md`: refresh phase status — standard authored (A), release mechanics + tag-only (A′/fixReleaseDocDirection), pokeedge conformance (B done), web conformance (#92), test-tier restructure in flight; mark Sonar retired; list the 4 followups.
- Verify: `actionlint` + self-test green; grep no Sonar references in security-gates CI configs.

### B. App-repo hygiene (cross-project: PokeEdge + PokeEdge-Web) — small plain-git PRs
- Remove `sonar-project.properties` from both repos.
- pokeedge: remove the `SONAR_TOKEN` legacy rows + Sonar mentions from `docs/CI_CD_PIPELINE.md` and `docs/code-quality-coverage.md`.
- Done as small reviewable PRs from worktrees (low collision risk — peer agents touch CI/test files, not Sonar config). No ADV checkpoint (cross-project); plain git. (AC1, partial AC2)

### C. Privileged ops — execute-if-scoped, else documented runbook + handoff (AC2, AC3)
- `SONAR_TOKEN` secret deletion: `gh secret delete SONAR_TOKEN --repo Sharper-Flow/PokeEdge` (+ `-Web`) — repo-admin; attempt with current token.
- **Stop analysis (SonarCloud + GitHub org owner — handoff):**
  1. SonarCloud: for each project (PokeEdge, PokeEdge-Web) → Administration → Analysis Method → disable Automatic Analysis.
  2. SonarCloud: remove each project's GitHub binding (org Administration → unbind), to avoid phantom bindings.
  3. GitHub org owner: uninstall the `sonarqubecloud` App (Org Settings → GitHub Apps → Configure → Uninstall).
  - Verify: `gh api orgs/Sharper-Flow/installations --jq '.installations[].app_slug'` no longer lists `sonarqubecloud`.
- These need SonarCloud admin + GitHub org-owner UI — NOT doable from this session; deliver as the AC3 runbook and hand off.

## Verification strategy
- Native: actionlint + self-test + grep clean (security-gates).
- App PRs: grep shows `sonar-project.properties` gone + no Sonar doc refs.
- Privileged: `gh api .../installations` no longer lists `sonarqubecloud`; SonarCloud projects show analysis disabled (runbook-verified by executor).

## Open risks
- If only files/secrets are removed but the App/Automatic Analysis stays, Sonar **keeps analyzing** (files are ignored) — retirement is NOT complete until step C is executed. The change's AC3 must not be marked satisfied by file removal alone.
- Phantom-binding gotcha: enforce the unbind-before-uninstall order in the runbook.
- Cross-project app-repo PRs must dodge peer-agent file conflicts (sonar files are isolated; low risk).
documents.executiveSummary: Fully retired SonarCloud across Sharper Flow. Background: the Sonar CI workflows had been removed earlier, but the `sonarqubecloud` GitHub App + `sonar-project.properties` + `SONAR_TOKEN` were still live, so SonarCloud Automatic Analysis kept running. This change completed the retirement: removed `sonar-project.properties` from both app repos (PRs #277 pokeedge, #94 web), deleted the `SONAR_TOKEN` secret from both, reframed pokeedge's CI/quality docs to 'SonarCloud retired', and reconciled the security-gates standard (`ci-standard.md` + `ROADMAP.md`) to state Sonar is retired with the four capability gaps tracked as research followups. The actual analysis-stop — uninstalling the `sonarqubecloud` GitHub App (which unbinds the SonarCloud org) — is an org-owner UI action not reachable via API (verified: the user PAT gets 404 on the org-installation DELETE); the org owner performed it, and `gh api orgs/Sharper-Flow/installations` confirms `sonarqubecloud` is no longer installed. A key verified subtlety drove the design: removing `sonar-project.properties` does NOT stop analysis (Sonar ignores it under Automatic Analysis) — only the App uninstall / Automatic-Analysis disable stops it, so the change deliberately blocked acceptance (AC3/DONT2 left `unknown`) until the App was actually gone. The four Sonar capabilities lost to retirement — duplication detection, maintainability/tech-debt metrics, coverage-on-new-code, and deep dataflow/taint SAST — are each tracked as a research→decide→implement fast-follow change (`addDuplicationDetection`, `addMaintainabilityMetrics`, `addDiffCoverageGate`, `deepenSastDataflow`); trends/dashboard/PR-decoration are intentionally dropped per the no-hosted-dashboard posture. Contract 10/10 (after the org-owner uninstall flipped AC3 + DONT2 to satisfied).

| ID | Kind | Requirement | Status | Evidence |

App uninstall unbinds the SonarCloud org → Automatic Analysis stopped. |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | **AC4** security-gates `ROADMAP.md` + `docs/ci-standard.md` reconciled: Sonar retired (current reality, not just a non-goal), phase status refreshed (B done, C done via #92, test-tier restructure in flight, tag-only release standard), and the 4 capability-gap followups recorded. | pass | ci-standard.md '## Code quality beyond the security gate' (Sonar retired + 4 followups table + out-by-posture); ROADMAP.md status refreshed + 'SonarCloud retirement' section + properties-doesn't-stop-analysis note. Committed (8e14797). |
| AC5 | acceptance_criterion | **AC5** No Sonar references remain in CI configs/workflows; `actionlint` + self-test green. | pass | actionlint PASS; docs state retired; app PRs remove properties; secrets gone; installations confirms App removed. |
| C1 | constraint | App disconnect, secret deletion, and app-repo file edits are privileged/cross-project ops — execute where the token + project context allow, else document the exact steps and hand off. | respected | Privileged ops handled per scope: secret deletion executed; App-disconnect runbook delivered + the org owner executed the uninstall. App-repo edits as isolated PRs. |
| C2 | constraint | Coordinate app-repo edits with the active peer agents in PokeEdge / PokeEdge-Web. | respected | No security-gate reusable workflow/composite changes; sonar-project.properties removal is safe (nothing in the required Sharperflow CI Gate path reads it). |
| C3 | constraint | Removing `sonar-project.properties` is safe — nothing in the required `Sharperflow CI Gate` path reads it. | respected | App-repo edits done as isolated PRs in fresh worktrees; no collision with peer-agent branches. |
| DONT1 | avoidance | Do not leave Sonar half-retired (App on, files off) — fully disconnect or document the exact remaining admin step. | respected | No Sonar capability silently dropped — duplication/maintainability/diff-coverage/deep-SAST tracked as the 4 fast-follow changes, recorded in ci-standard.md + ROADMAP.md. |
| DONT2 | avoidance | Do not drop a wanted Sonar capability without its tracked followup (the 4 fast-follows cover duplication, maintainability, diff-coverage, deep SAST). | respected | RESOLVED: no longer half-retired — the sonarqubecloud App is uninstalled AND sonar-project.properties/SONAR_TOKEN removed. Full retirement achieved (App gone confirmed via installations list). |
